metadata.official_name: Missouri University of Science and Technology

strategic_profile.executive_summary: Missouri University of Science and Technology (Missouri S&T) is a STEM-dominant, public R1 research university located in Rolla, Missouri [cite: 1]. Known for its exceptional return on investment and high career placement rates, the university mandates experiential learning for all undergraduates [cite: 2]. While heavily focused on engineering and computing, it maintains a close-knit, moderately selective academic environment [cite: 3, 4].
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic for test-optional applicants; Numbers-focused for test-submitters [cite: 5]

analyst_takeaways.insight: 100% of undergraduate students are required to participate in experiential learning, such as design teams, research, or co-ops [cite: 2].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students are guaranteed hands-on engineering or scientific experience, significantly boosting employability.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Missouri S&T has implemented a 'plateau' tuition model for undergraduates taking 12-18 credit hours, combined with differential tuition based on major [cite: 6, 7].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students are financially incentivized to take 15 credit hours per semester to graduate faster without paying extra per credit.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The campus has a heavily male-dominated demographic (around 76% men) and a strong Greek life presence, which serves as a major social anchor in the small town of Rolla [cite: 8, 9].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should be prepared for a highly specialized, technically-minded peer group where social life often revolves around campus organizations and design teams.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Social life is heavily influenced by Greek life and over 200 student clubs, including highly competitive student design teams (e.g., Mars Rover, Solar Car) [cite: 4, 9, 10]. The St. Pat's celebration is a legendary campus tradition [cite: 9].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in the rural college town of Rolla, MO, surrounded by the Ozark Mountains. While providing a safe and close-knit environment, the distance from major urban centers can necessitate proactive networking for certain non-STEM fields [cite: 11, 12].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 institution by Carnegie (very high research activity), S&T heavily integrates undergraduates into research across its many institutes, including the National Institute for Mine Safety and Health Research [cite: 1, 13].

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for Fall 2025/2026. Applicants opting out of test scores are evaluated holistically by a committee, which requires additional information on extracurriculars and community impact. Test-optional applicants may have limited merit scholarship opportunities [cite: 5, 14].

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed; institution reportedly does not use an admissions waitlist [cite: 15].

gpa.notes: GPA is highly considered; an average admitted student holds a 3.80 high school GPA [cite: 16].

testing.policy_note: 13% of accepted students submit SAT scores; 78% submit ACT scores [cite: 3].

colleges.name: College of Engineering and Computing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students committed to traditional and cutting-edge engineering fields. Freshmen enter a common 'Freshman Engineering' program before formally applying to a specific department [cite: 17].
colleges.housing_profile: Standard campus housing; Residential College and Thomas Jefferson Hall are popular [cite: 18].
colleges.student_archetype: Technically minded, problem-solver interested in hands-on design teams.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Requires successful completion of freshman engineering/science requirements before formal admission to the department [cite: 17].

core_courses: COMP SCI 1010 Introduction To Computer Science
COMP SCI 1200 Discrete Mathematics for Computer Science
COMP SCI 1570 Introduction To C++ Programming
electives: COMP SCI 5408 Game Theory for Computing

majors.notes: Students can apply for the Graduate Track Pathway (GTP) to accelerate a B.S. to M.S. degree, double-counting up to 9 credit hours [cite: 19, 20].

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.notes: This is historically the most popular major at the university, producing the highest number of graduates [cite: 18].

colleges.name: College of Arts, Sciences, and Education
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Geared toward students interested in the physical sciences, biological sciences, humanities, or secondary education infused with a technological perspective [cite: 1, 21].

colleges.name: Kummer College of Innovation, Entrepreneurship, and Economic Development
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students wanting to blend STEM knowledge with business, management, or entrepreneurial skills [cite: 22].

application_deadlines.notes: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Priority deadline for Fall admission is April 1 [cite: 16, 23].

supplemental_requirements.details: Test-optional applicants must provide short answer essays detailing extracurricular activities and how they will contribute to the Missouri S&T community [cite: 5, 24].

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: The committee places significant weight on core math and science coursework (Algebra I through Calculus, Physics, Chemistry). Test-optional candidates must show clear evidence of STEM passion through extracurriculars [cite: 14, 25].

major_selection_tactics: Apply to the common Freshman Engineering program if undecided among engineering disciplines, as it allows students to explore before formally declaring [cite: 17].
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Students unsuccessful in direct engineering transfer pathways can major in physical sciences or mathematics, provided they maintain the baseline 2.5 GPA required to transfer into an engineering department later [cite: 26].

financials.tuition_model: Missouri S&T utilizes a differential tuition model based on the student's major, combined with a 'plateau' (flat) rate for undergraduates taking 12-18 credit hours, incentivizing students to take 15 hours per semester [cite: 6, 7].

scholarships.name: Missouri Groundbreaker Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable award for Missouri residents based on GPA and test scores [cite: 27].

scholarships.name: Out-of-State Groundbreaker Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable award for non-Missouri residents based on GPA and test scores [cite: 27].

scholarships.name: Provost's International Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable scholarship for high-achieving international students [cite: 27].

scholarships.name: Trailblazer Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to first-generation college students who meet merit criteria [cite: 27].

scholarships.name: Academic Pathways Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to domestic freshmen studying in the CASE or KUMMER Colleges [cite: 27].

ap_policy.general_rule: Missouri S&T recognizes advanced credit for participating high school students who achieve sufficient scores on AP exams [cite: 28, 29].
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to place out of degree-specific course requirements and earn actual college credit.

ib_policy.general_rule: Recognizes the IB Diploma and grants course credit and advanced placement for standard-level and higher-level exams (e.g., minimum score of 4 in Biology) [cite: 30].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: The last 60 hours of an undergraduate program must be taken in residence at Missouri S&T. A maximum of 15 hours of the last 60 may be taken off-campus with provost approval [cite: 26, 31].

what_it_takes: A strong foundation in advanced mathematics (Calculus) and physical sciences (Physics, Chemistry) [cite: 5].
Ability to handle rigorous and highly demanding technical coursework [cite: 16].
A willingness to participate in hands-on, experiential learning projects [cite: 2].
common_activities: Student Design Teams (Mars Rover, Solar Car, Mine Rescue) [cite: 10, 32]
St. Pat's Board (St. Patrick's Day celebration organizing) [cite: 9]
Society of Women Engineers (SWE) and other professional societies [cite: 33]
essay_tips: Detail concrete ways you will contribute to the S&T campus community [cite: 24].
Focus on leadership and how specific activities have shaped your problem-solving abilities [cite: 24].
Use bullet-point outlines to craft clear, concise answers to short-answer prompts [cite: 34].
red_flags: Lack of demonstrated interest in STEM fields (for test-optional candidates) [cite: 35].
Inadequate preparation in high school mathematics (below Algebra II/Trig) [cite: 14].
insights: Students consistently highlight that coursework is notoriously difficult and exams have low averages, designed to prepare students for real-world industry standards rather than inflate GPAs [cite: 16].
The campus is located in a rural setting, which naturally pushes social life toward on-campus clubs, design teams, and Greek organizations [cite: 9].
